Beneath or beside these, tuck subtle text links for alternative flows (e.g., 'View detailed pricing', 'Request a custom quote'). In the pricing transparency panel below, the handler controls a row or grid of smaller, evenly spaced link tiles or buttons arranged horizontally across the card’s lower half. All of these CTAs share consistent spacing (16–24px around and between) and align to the right or center of their respective containers, reinforcing the downward and rightward visual flow toward conversion.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Inline within both the service-detail-section and pricing-transparency-panel; CTAs reposition responsively (stacking vertically and centering on mobile) but remain anchored to the lower portion of their containers, never becoming sticky or fixed.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The pricing transparency panel appears directly below the large orange services card, separated from it by a generous vertical gap of about 48–64px, still within the same white background section. It is a wide, horizontally oriented rounded rectangle card, centered within the max-width container and spanning roughly 70–80% of its width, visually echoing the size and alignment of the main services card but with slightly less height (about 25–35% of the viewport on desktop). The panel’s background may use a complementary brand color—either a softer orange or a green accent—to differentiate it from the main card while maintaining a cohesive look. Internally, there is ample padding (24–32px on all sides). The top-left area contains a concise heading such as 'Transparent Pricing & Next Steps' with a brief explanatory line beneath. Below and/or to the right, a structured set of link blocks or buttons guide users to 'Instant Quote', 'Cost Estimator', 'Full Pricing', and 'Book Now' flows. These links are arranged in one or two rows, centered or right-aligned, with equal spacing (16–24px) between them, and managed by the service-navigation-cta-handler. Overall, the panel acts as a clear, secondary decision zone, visually lighter than the main services card but still prominent enough to attract attention after the user has browsed service details.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Inline, stacked directly after the main services card; it scales with viewport width but maintains its centered position and rounded shape. On smaller screens, its internal layout shifts to a vertical stack of heading, description, and CTAs while preserving generous padding and clear separation from surrounding content.

name: About Us
title: About Us
description: This page retrieves and displays dynamic company background, mission, credentials, and customer testimonials using the getAboutUsContent interface, and provides a direct navigation path to the Contact page.

core_features: Load company background data (company_name, mission, history, credentials, testimonials) from the getAboutUsContent interface when the page is opened.
Display a structured company overview section that shows the company name, mission, and history from the API response.
Display a credentials section listing license, insurance, experience, and other trust-related credentials from the credentials array returned by the API.
Display a testimonials section using the testimonials array, with support for simple client-side filtering by rating and source, based on URL parameters.
Provide internal section navigation (Overview, Credentials, Testimonials, Contact) controlled by a URL parameter so users can deep-link to specific sections.

Provide a “Contact us” call-to-action that navigates to contact.html with the topic URL parameter set to general as specified, optionally preserving the current About Us context via additional URL parameters.
Handle loading and error states for getAboutUsContent with retry behavior, without using hardcoded fallback data.
user_workflows:
View company overview:
1) User navigates to about.html (from footer link or a direct link).
2) On page initialization, the about-content-loader component calls getAboutUsContent with no parameters.
3) While waiting, a loading state is shown instead of content.
4) Once data is returned, the company-overview-section component displays company_name, mission, and history.
5) The section-navigation component scrolls or focuses to the section specified in the URL parameter section (if present), otherwise to the overview section.

Inspect credentials for trust:
1) User opens about.html or is already on the page.
2) The credentials-list component receives the credentials array from about-content-loader.
3) Each credential’s label and value are rendered as a list.
4) If the user clicks a section-navigation link that sets section=credentials in the URL, the page updates the URL and scrolls/focuses the credentials section so it is immediately visible.

Browse and refine testimonials:
1) User opens about.html; getAboutUsContent loads testimonials.
2) The testimonials-filter-controls component reads URL parameters minRating, source, and testimonialsPage (if present) and initializes its controls accordingly.
3) The testimonials-list component applies filters to the testimonials array in-memory: it filters out testimonials that do not meet the minRating or source URL parameters and applies pagination based on testimonialsPage.
4) When the user changes a filter (e.g., selects a different minimum rating or source), testimonials-filter-controls updates the URL parameters (minRating, source, testimonialsPage) and emits an event.
5) testimonials-list reacts to the updated URL parameters, recomputes the filtered + paginated list, and re-renders the testimonials without making any additional API calls.

Navigate directly to a specific section via URL:
1) A user clicks a link from another page pointing to about.html?section=testimonials.
2) On load, about-content-loader calls getAboutUsContent.
3) After data is loaded, section-navigation reads section=testimonials from the URL and instructs the page to scroll/focus the testimonials section.
4) If the user changes the section using in-page navigation, section-navigation updates the section URL parameter and scrolls accordingly.

Go from About Us to Contact page:
1) User reads content and decides to reach out.
2) User clicks the Contact CTA button rendered by contact-cta.
3) contact-cta constructs a URL for contact.html including the required parameter topic=general and an optional ref=about (and may optionally pass section or other non-conflicting context parameters).
4) Browser navigates to contact.html?topic=general&ref=about.
5) The Contact page can use the topic parameter as needed (outside the scope of this page).
interactions: Page initialization: When about.html loads, the about-content-loader component automatically triggers the getAboutUsContent interface call (onInit). During this interaction, the page shows a loading state and disables any content that depends on the API response.

Loading error handling: If getAboutUsContent fails, about-content-loader transitions to an error state and shows a generic error message plus a Retry button. Clicking Retry triggers another call to getAboutUsContent (without any parameters) and re-attempts to populate all dependent components.

Section navigation: Clicking a section link (e.g., Overview, Credentials, Testimonials, Contact Info) in section-navigation updates the URL parameter section (e.g., ?section=testimonials) using the History API and scrolls/focuses the corresponding content section. The component also reacts to browser back/forward events (popstate) to keep the visible section in sync with the section URL parameter.

Testimonials filtering: Interactions with testimonials-filter-controls (changing minimum rating, changing testimonial source, or resetting filters) update the relevant URL parameters (minRating, source, testimonialsPage). This triggers testimonials-list to recompute its filtered list based solely on the already-loaded testimonials array.

Testimonials pagination: When the user clicks Next or Previous in testimonials-list, the component updates the testimonialsPage URL parameter, recalculates which slice of the testimonials array to show, and re-renders the list. It also listens to popstate to update the displayed page when the user navigates back/forward.

Contact CTA navigation: Clicking the Contact CTA button in contact-cta performs a client-side navigation to contact.html with topic=general plus any additional non-conflicting context parameters (e.g., ref=about). This uses a standard link or programmatic navigation and does not affect or require any additional API calls on this page.
page_functionality.state_logic:
The About Us page state is driven entirely by the URL parameters and the response from getAboutUsContent. There is no use of localStorage or other client-side persistent storage.

Relevant URL parameters:
1) section: Controls which content section should be considered active on load and when changed via in-page navigation. Supported values might include 'overview', 'credentials', 'testimonials', and 'contact'.
   - On initial load: The page reads section. If present and recognized, section-navigation scrolls/focuses that section after getAboutUsContent data has been loaded. If not present or unrecognized, 'overview' is used as the default logical section, without altering the URL.
   - On user navigation: section-navigation updates the section parameter using History API when a section tab/link is clicked and then scrolls/focuses the selected section.
   - On browser back/forward: section-navigation listens to popstate and re-applies the section parameter by scrolling/focusing the appropriate section.

2) minRating: Controls client-side filtering of testimonials based on rating.
   - On initial load: testimonials-filter-controls reads minRating from the URL (if present) and initializes the minimum rating filter. testimonials-list then filters the testimonials array, only showing items where testimonial.rating is greater than or equal to this numeric value.
   - On filter changes: When the user updates the minRating in testimonials-filter-controls, it updates the minRating parameter in the URL and resets testimonialsPage to the first page. testimonials-list re-filters the in-memory testimonials array accordingly.

3) source: Controls client-side filtering of testimonials based on testimonial.source.
   - On initial load: testimonials-filter-controls reads source from the URL if present. testimonials-list filters testimonials to only those where testimonial.source matches the source parameter.
   - On filter changes: When the user changes or clears the source filter, testimonials-filter-controls updates the source parameter in the URL (adding, changing, or removing it) and resets testimonialsPage. testimonials-list re-calculates the filtered list.

4) testimonialsPage: Controls client-side pagination for testimonials.
   - On initial load: testimonials-list reads testimonialsPage from the URL. If absent or invalid, it defaults to page 1 (logically) without altering the URL. It computes which subset of the currently filtered testimonials to display.
   - On pagination clicks: Clicking Next/Previous updates the testimonialsPage parameter in the URL and triggers recomputation of the current slice of testimonials.
   - On filter changes: testimonials-filter-controls resets testimonialsPage to the first page when filters change, updating the URL parameter accordingly.
   - On browser back/forward: testimonials-list listens for changes in testimonialsPage via popstate and updates the visible subset.

5) Other parameters: Any extra parameters (such as ref) that may be included in links to about.html are preserved on navigation where appropriate but do not affect the core logic above.

The getAboutUsContent interface does not accept parameters; it is always called once per page load by about-content-loader. All stateful behavior (section navigation, testimonial filters, pagination) is implemented purely on the client using the single API response and URL parameters.

components.functionality: Responsible for calling the getAboutUsContent interface when the page initializes, managing loading and error states, and providing the resulting data object (company_name, mission, history, credentials, testimonials) to all dependent components via a shared state or context.
event_handlers: onInit: Triggered when about.html is loaded. Calls getAboutUsContent with no parameters and sets an internal loading state until a response is received.
onSuccess: Triggered when getAboutUsContent returns successfully. Stores the response in component state/context and clears the loading flag so dependent components can render.
onError: Triggered if getAboutUsContent fails. Stores an error state to display a generic error message and shows a Retry action.
onRetryClick: Triggered when the user clicks a Retry button in the error state. Re-invokes getAboutUsContent and reinitializes loading/error states.

components.functionality: Provides navigation controls (e.g., tabs or links) to jump between logical sections of the About Us content: overview, credentials, testimonials, and contact info. Keeps the active section synchronized with the section URL parameter and scrolls/focuses the corresponding section.
event_handlers: onInit: Reads the section URL parameter and sets the initial active section. After about-content-loader finishes loading, scrolls/focuses the target section if specified.
onSectionLinkClick: When a user clicks a navigation item, updates the section URL parameter using the History API and scrolls/focuses the corresponding content section.
onPopState: Listens for browser back/forward events and updates the active section based on the current section URL parameter, re-scrolling/focusing as needed.

components.functionality: Displays the core company overview information, including company_name, mission, and history, as returned by getAboutUsContent. This section is typically the default landing area when visiting about.html without a section parameter.
event_handlers: onRender: Renders overview content only when about-content-loader has successfully loaded data. If loading is in progress, shows a loading indication; if in error state, shows an appropriate message or hides the section.
onSectionActivated: Invoked by section-navigation when section=overview becomes active. Ensures the section is scrolled into view and focused for accessibility.

components.functionality: Renders the list of company credentials (licenses, insurance details, years in business, certifications, etc.) from the credentials array returned by getAboutUsContent, helping users verify professionalism and trustworthiness.
event_handlers: onRender: Iterates over the credentials array and displays each credential using its label and value properties once data is loaded. If credentials is empty, shows a non-data-specific message indicating that credentials are not available.
onSectionActivated: Invoked by section-navigation when section=credentials becomes active. Scrolls/focuses this section into view.
onCredentialClick: Optional interaction if individual credentials are clickable (e.g., to expand or show more detail). Toggling this state does not trigger any new API calls.

components.functionality: Provides user controls to refine which testimonials are shown based on attributes such as minimum rating and source. Keeps filter state synchronized with URL parameters and triggers updates in testimonials-list.
event_handlers: onInit: Reads minRating and source from the URL parameters (if present) and initializes the filter control values to match.

onMinRatingChange: When the user selects a different minimum rating, updates the minRating URL parameter (adding or modifying it) and resets testimonialsPage to the first page. Emits an event to notify testimonials-list of filter changes.

onSourceChange: When the user changes the testimonial source filter, updates the source URL parameter (adding, changing, or removing it) and resets testimonialsPage. Emits an event to notify testimonials-list.
onResetFiltersClick: Clears filter controls, removes minRating and source from the URL, resets testimonialsPage, and emits an event to re-render the full testimonials list.

components.functionality: Displays customer testimonials returned by getAboutUsContent, applying client-side filtering and pagination based on URL parameters minRating, source, and testimonialsPage. Helps users understand others' experiences and build trust.
event_handlers: onRender: Applies filters and pagination to getAboutUsContent.testimonials whenever data or relevant URL parameters change. Filtering is done by comparing testimonial.rating with minRating and testimonial.source with source. Pagination uses testimonialsPage to compute which subset to display.
onNextPageClick: Increments the testimonialsPage URL parameter (if another page of testimonials exists) and re-renders the list using the updated subset, without making additional API calls.
onPreviousPageClick: Decrements the testimonialsPage URL parameter when possible and re-renders the appropriate subset of testimonials.

onFilterChanged: Triggered when testimonials-filter-controls emits a filter change. Resets internal pagination state to align with the updated testimonialsPage URL parameter (usually page 1) and re-renders the filtered testimonials.
onSectionActivated: Invoked by section-navigation when section=testimonials becomes active. Scrolls/focuses the testimonials section into view.
onPopState: Listens for browser back/forward navigation and updates the currently displayed testimonials subset according to the current minRating, source, and testimonialsPage URL parameters.

components.functionality: Provides a prominent action entry point for users to contact the moving company from the About Us page. Navigates to contact.html with the required topic URL parameter set to general and may append additional context parameters such as ref=about.
event_handlers: onRender: Ensures the CTA is only displayed when the page is not in a blocking error state that would prevent normal navigation (although navigation can still technically function).

onClick: Constructs a navigation URL to contact.html including at minimum topic=general. It may also include ref=about or other non-conflicting context parameters from the current URL. Performs a full-page navigation or link redirect to the constructed URL.
onSectionActivated: Invoked by section-navigation when section=contact becomes active. Scrolls/focuses the contact-cta area so the user’s attention is drawn to the ability to reach out.

layout.grid_system: Implicit 12-column responsive grid with a centered max-width content container (likely around 1140–1200px) and large outer gutters.
layout.layout_pattern: The page uses a linear, top-to-bottom flow inside a centered max-width container (~1140–1200px). Above everything (outside this spec) is the global floating nav and dark hero band with the 'About Us' title and a short intro. Immediately below the hero, the About page content begins on a white background: first a floating-style local section navigation card, then the company overview content, followed by a large, wide, orange rounded card highlighting company credentials. Beneath this, a new band introduces the testimonials area (still centered within the same max-width), starting with filter controls and then a stacked list of testimonials. At the very bottom, a bold contact CTA button is centered with generous surrounding whitespace, clearly linking to the Contact page.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: The page uses a linear, top-to-bottom flow inside a centered max-width container (~1140–1200px). Above everything (outside this spec) is the global floating nav and dark hero band with the 'About Us' title and a short intro. Immediately below the hero, the About page content begins on a white background: first a floating-style local section navigation card, then the company overview content, followed by a large, wide, orange rounded card highlighting company credentials. Beneath this, a new band introduces the testimonials area (still centered within the same max-width), starting with filter controls and then a stacked list of testimonials. At the very bottom, a bold contact CTA button is centered with generous surrounding whitespace, clearly linking to the Contact page.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: The hero above takes roughly 45–55% of the first viewport height, while this About content occupies the remaining scroll. The main content column sits centered with large left/right gutters (10–15% of viewport width each on desktop). Vertical spacing between major sections (overview, credentials, testimonials, CTA) is generous—around 64–96px margin between sections, and 40–64px padding inside main content groups. The orange credentials card and testimonials area each occupy around 70–80% of the main content width, while lists inside them are spaced with 16–24px vertical gaps to keep content readable and airy.
layout_strategies.content_density: Content density is low to medium. Each section focuses on a single purpose: overview storytelling, credentials proof, testimonials social proof, and finally the contact CTA. Text blocks are short, headings are prominent, and lists are broken into visually distinct items (cards or rows) with 16–24px padding and 24–32px spacing between them. There is ample negative space around and between components to maintain the clean, marketing-oriented feel from the mockup.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: The user’s eye moves from the hero’s central 'About Us' heading down to the floating local section navigation card, which acts as a table of contents for the page. From there, the left-aligned company overview heading anchors the start of the content narrative, flowing downward into body copy. The bright orange credentials card below becomes the next strong visual attractor, pulling attention to trust signals. As the user scrolls, the eye lands on the testimonials filter controls (pills or buttons) and then moves directly into the testimonials list beneath. The journey concludes at the bottom with a highly prominent, centrally aligned contact CTA button that echoes the hero’s accent colors, guiding users toward the Contact page.

layout.overall_description: The About Us page sits under the same dark, full-width hero band as the rest of the site, with a centered page title and intro copy. Just as the hero ends, the background shifts to solid white and the main About content appears inside a centered, max-width container. At the very top of this white area, a rounded, floating-style section navigation card spans roughly three-quarters of the content width, visually echoing the global nav’s pill-like shape. Below it, the page transitions into a storytelling band: a left-aligned 'Our Company' or 'Who We Are' heading with supporting paragraphs stacked beneath, all aligned to the left edge of the content column but centered within the overall page. As the reader scrolls, a large, bright orange rounded rectangle card fills most of the horizontal space, sitting like a prominent panel in the middle of the white background. This card houses the company’s credentials, arranged in a clean, lightly bordered list or grid. The next band seamlessly continues the white background but feels like its own section thanks to generous top margin and a section title for testimonials. At the top of this testimonials band, horizontally centered filter controls (e.g., pill buttons) float above a vertical stack of testimonial cards, each with ample padding and breathing room. The journey concludes with a single, bold, full-width (on mobile) but constrained-width (on desktop) contact CTA button centered near the bottom, surrounded by generous whitespace. The overall impression is a modern, friendly corporate experience that guides visitors smoothly from understanding the company to trusting it, and finally to contacting it.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The data loader is not visible as static content but occupies the same central content column as the rest of the About page. When loading, it overlays a semi-transparent dark veil over the company overview, credentials, and testimonials area—from just below the section navigation card to just above the final contact CTA button—spanning approximately 80% of the page width (aligned to the centered max-width container). A circular spinner or subtle loading indicator sits exactly in the center of this region, both horizontally and vertically, maintaining the minimal, modern aesthetic while clearly signaling that the page content in this band is still being fetched.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Behaves as an overlay within the main content area: when active, it is positioned absolutely relative to the main content container, covering the sections below the hero while allowing the global nav and hero background to remain visible above. When inactive, it collapses and takes up no visual space.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The local section navigation appears immediately below the hero band, at the very top of the white content area. It is a horizontally oriented, pill-shaped card centered in the page, spanning roughly 70–80% of the available content width, with large rounded corners and a discreet shadow to mimic the main navigation’s floating look. Inside, section labels (e.g., 'Overview', 'Credentials', 'Testimonials') are arranged left to right as evenly spaced text links or pill buttons, aligned vertically in the middle of the card. There is 24–32px padding inside the card vertically and 32–40px horizontally. Above it, there is about 48–64px of whitespace separating it from the bottom of the hero; below it, around 48px of vertical space before the company overview heading begins, to keep the layout airy.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Initially static within the flow but becomes sticky once the user scrolls past its original position: it pins to a short distance below the top of the viewport (just beneath the global nav) while remaining centered horizontally, preserving its floating card appearance and giving users persistent local navigation across the About sections.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The company overview section sits directly below the section navigation card, inside the same white background band. It is aligned to the main content column centered on the page, with a max width of about 800–900px (slightly narrower than the full container to improve readability). A bold section heading (e.g., 'Our Story' or 'Who We Are') is left-aligned at the top of this block, starting roughly 48px below the section navigation card. The heading occupies about one line and sits near the left edge of the content column, leaving generous whitespace to its right. Below the heading, one to three paragraphs of body text are stacked, also left-aligned, with 16–24px spacing between them. Overall vertical padding for this block is about 48–64px top and bottom, and there is a clear 64–80px gap below the last paragraph before the large orange credentials card begins.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static within the normal document flow, scaling responsively: on wider screens it remains a single, narrow text column; on smaller screens it approaches full-width but maintains consistent left alignment and generous vertical spacing.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The credentials list is housed inside a large, bright orange rounded rectangle card that visually echoes the prominent form card in the Contact page mockup. This card sits centered below the company overview section on the same white background, spanning about 80% of the content width (roughly 900–1000px on desktop), with large radiused corners and a subtle inner shadow or elevation. There is a 64px vertical gap between the bottom of the overview text and the top of this card, giving it room to stand out. Inside the card, credentials are presented as a clean list or grid: for example, two columns on desktop, collapsing to a single column on smaller screens. Each credential item is a row with an icon or badge on the left and short text to the right, spaced with 16–24px vertical gaps between items and 24px padding around the edges of the card. The card’s orange background contrasts strongly with the white page background, making the credentials an obvious trust-focused focal point in the middle of the page.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static and centered in the layout. Responsively, it maintains its rounded card shape: on large screens it stays at ~80% of the container width; on tablets and mobile it expands toward nearly full width while preserving internal padding and the vertical stacking of credentials.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The testimonials filter controls sit at the top of the testimonials area, which begins just below the orange credentials card. There is approximately 80–96px of vertical space between the bottom of the credentials card and the start of this testimonials band, creating a clear visual transition while keeping the same white background. At the top center of this area, a concise section heading like 'Customer Testimonials' appears, left-aligned within the content column but placed centrally in the overall page width. Directly beneath this heading (about 16–24px of vertical space), the filter controls form a horizontal row of pill-shaped buttons or tabs, centered horizontally. The row spans roughly 60–70% of the content width, with individual filters evenly spaced and padded (8–12px vertical, 16–24px horizontal per pill). Active filters may be highlighted with an orange or green fill and white text, consistent with the brand accents. Generous whitespace (24–32px) separates the filter controls from the first testimonial card below.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static within the testimonials section. The filter row remains centered and, on smaller screens, wraps gracefully into two rows while preserving pill shapes and consistent spacing between controls.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The testimonials list appears directly beneath the filter controls within the same testimonials band. It is constrained to the central content column, spanning about 70–80% of the container width, and is vertically stacked. Each testimonial is displayed in its own card-like container: a white or very light card on the white background, differentiated with a subtle border or shadow and rounded corners. Cards have ample internal padding (24px all around) and 16–24px vertical spacing between them. Within each card, the testimonial quote is the primary element, followed by the customer name, and possibly a small location or rating indicator. On desktop, the list may appear as a single column; alternately, on very wide screens, it can become a two-column grid while preserving the overall generous spacing. There is a clear 64–80px vertical gap from the last testimonial card down to the contact CTA button below, ensuring the list feels substantial but not cramped.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static, scrolling content. The list grows vertically as more testimonials are loaded or filtered, pushing the contact CTA downward but preserving consistent top and bottom margins within the testimonials band.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The contact CTA button is the closing element of the About page’s main content, anchored at the bottom center of the page below the testimonials list. It is placed with a generous 64–80px vertical gap above it (from the last testimonial card), and an equally generous 80–96px of whitespace below before the site footer, creating a strong, standalone visual moment. The button is large and horizontally oriented, with a pill-like shape that echoes the navigation and credentials card styling. On desktop, it spans about 30–40% of the content width (e.g., 280–360px), centered in the page; on mobile, it can stretch closer to full width minus side gutters. The button uses a bold brand color (likely the same orange as the credentials card) with white text and clear, legible label such as 'Contact Us' or 'Get a Free Quote'.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static within the layout, positioned as the final call-to-action in the normal scroll flow. On smaller screens, it remains anchored to the content bottom rather than fixed to the viewport, preserving the clean, non-intrusive feel of the page while still being highly noticeable due to its color and generous surrounding whitespace.

name: Service Areas
title: Service Areas
description: Page for checking whether a ZIP code is covered, reviewing the list of service areas, and moving on to quote requests or bookings when coverage is available.

core_features: Fetch and display the list of service areas (cities, neighborhoods, ZIP codes) using getServiceAreasList.
Show the configured local move radius in miles using getServiceAreasList.local_move_radius_miles.
Allow users to enter a ZIP code and check coverage using checkZipCoverage.
Automatically run a coverage check when the page is opened with a zip URL parameter and highlight the matching area in the list.
Display coverage results, including the API-provided message and allowed move scopes, based on checkZipCoverage.
If the ZIP is covered for local moves, provide direct navigation to quote.html and booking.html with appropriate URL parameters.
If the ZIP is not covered, direct users to contact.html with a topic parameter for service area questions.
user_workflows:
Workflow 1 – Browse service areas without a ZIP:
1. User opens areas.html without any URL parameters.
2. Page-init controller calls getServiceAreasList and stores the result for the session.
3. Service area list component displays the cities, neighborhoods, and ZIPs from the API response.
4. Local radius summary component shows the local_move_radius_miles value from the API.
5. User can scroll the list or click a ZIP from the list to run a coverage check and see available actions.

Workflow 2 – Check ZIP coverage from a direct link:
1. User arrives at areas.html with a zip URL parameter (e.g., areas.html?zip=XXXXX) from index.html or quote.html.
2. Page-init controller reads the zip parameter.
3. Page-init controller calls getServiceAreasList and, in parallel or immediately afterward, calls checkZipCoverage with the zip value.
4. Coverage result panel displays the message, coverage status, and allowed move scopes from checkZipCoverage.
5. Service area list highlights the area section that contains the ZIP from the URL (matching against the zip_codes arrays from getServiceAreasList).
6. If coverage includes local moves, area action links are enabled for quote and booking; if not, a contact link is shown instead.

Workflow 3 – Manually enter a ZIP and proceed to quote:
1. User opens areas.html (with or without an existing zip parameter).
2. User types a ZIP code into the ZIP coverage form.
3. On form submit, the component updates the page URL to areas.html?zip=EnteredZip and triggers checkZipCoverage with the entered ZIP.
4. Coverage result panel updates with the new checkZipCoverage result.
5. Service area list scrolls or highlights the area that contains the entered ZIP, if present in the getServiceAreasList data.
6. If is_covered is true and allowed_move_scopes includes "local", the user clicks “Get a quote for this area”.
7. Browser navigates to quote.html with URL parameters move_scope=local and placeholders for other parameters as configured, e.g., quote.html?move_scope=local&home_size=&move_date=&max_hourly_rate=.

Workflow 4 – Manually enter a ZIP and proceed to booking:
1. User enters a ZIP code in the ZIP coverage form and submits.
2. Component updates the zip URL parameter and calls checkZipCoverage.
3. Coverage result panel shows that the area is covered and displays allowed_move_scopes including "local".
4. User clicks “Book a move in this area”.
5. Browser navigates to booking.html with URL parameters such as booking.html?source=direct&move_date= (move_date left empty or to be filled on the booking page).

Workflow 5 – Uncovered ZIP leading to contact:
1. User arrives with a zip parameter or submits the ZIP coverage form.
2. checkZipCoverage returns is_covered=false (or does not include the desired move scope in allowed_move_scopes).
3. Coverage result panel displays the message from the API, indicating limitations.
4. Panel shows a primary action link or button “Contact us about service availability”.
5. Browser navigates to contact.html with at least topic=service_area in the query string (e.g., contact.html?topic=service_area), and may optionally include the zip parameter for context if supported by the contact page.
interactions: On page load, the controller triggers getServiceAreasList to populate the service area list and local radius summary.
On page load, if a zip URL parameter exists, the controller calls checkZipCoverage with that ZIP and passes the result to the coverage result panel and the service area list for highlighting.
When the user types into the ZIP input field, the form updates its internal value but does not request coverage until submission.
When the user submits the ZIP coverage form, the component updates the browser URL to include zip=EnteredZip and triggers checkZipCoverage with the entered ZIP.

When checkZipCoverage completes, the coverage result panel updates to show the coverage status, allowed_move_scopes, and message, and toggles which action links (quote, booking, contact) are available.
When the user clicks on a ZIP within the service area list, the list component updates the URL’s zip parameter and triggers checkZipCoverage for that ZIP.

When the user clicks “Get a quote for this area” from the coverage result panel, the browser navigates to quote.html with the configured query parameters, including move_scope=local and empty placeholders for home_size, move_date, and max_hourly_rate.

When the user clicks “Book a move in this area” from the coverage result panel, the browser navigates to booking.html with the configured query parameters, including source=direct and an empty move_date placeholder.
When the user clicks “Contact us about service availability” (for an uncovered or restricted ZIP), the browser navigates to contact.html with topic=service_area in the query string.
page_functionality.state_logic: The primary state for this page is derived from URL parameters and API responses only, without using localStorage. The main URL parameter is zip. When areas.html loads, the page-init controller reads window.location.search to determine if a zip parameter is present. If zip is present and non-empty, the page calls checkZipCoverage(zip) and stores the result in in-memory page state for the current view. The same zip value is also used by the service area list component to highlight any area whose zip_codes collection from getServiceAreasList contains that ZIP. When the user submits the ZIP coverage form or clicks a ZIP in the service area list, the component updates the browser URL to areas.html?zip=SelectedZip (replacing or adding the zip parameter) via a full page navigation or history API, ensuring the current state can be bookmarked and shared. The page-init logic re-runs on navigation, making the ZIP coverage and highlighting reproducible from the URL alone. The getServiceAreasList response is fetched on each page load and used to display the list and the local_move_radius_miles value. The coverage result panel and actionable links (to quote, booking, contact) are purely derived from the latest checkZipCoverage response and the current zip parameter. No hardcoded service area values are stored; all display content about areas, ZIPs, and radius comes directly from getServiceAreasList and checkZipCoverage.

components.functionality: Coordinates initial data loading and URL-driven behavior. On page load, reads the zip URL parameter, calls getServiceAreasList to fetch the list of service areas, and, if zip is present, calls checkZipCoverage with that ZIP. Stores the responses in in-memory state to provide data to other components, and triggers highlighting of the matching area.

Allows the user to enter a ZIP code to check whether it is within the service area. On submit, it updates the URL’s zip parameter and triggers a coverage check using checkZipCoverage. It also can prefill the input field with the current URL zip value, if present.

Displays the current ZIP coverage result based on checkZipCoverage and the zip URL parameter. Shows whether the ZIP is covered, the API-provided message, and the allowed_move_scopes. Based on these values, it conditionally exposes action links: quote and booking actions when local moves are allowed, or a contact action when the ZIP is not covered or local service is not available.

Handles navigation to quote.html, booking.html, and contact.html based on the current coverage state. When invoked from the coverage-result-panel, constructs appropriate URLs with query parameters and triggers browser navigation. For covered ZIPs where allowed_move_scopes includes "local", it navigates to quote.html with move_scope=local, home_size=, move_date=, max_hourly_rate=, and to booking.html with source=direct and move_date=. For uncovered or restricted ZIPs, it navigates to contact.html with topic=service_area and may include the zip parameter if supported.

Displays the structured list of service areas returned by getServiceAreasList, organized by city and including neighborhoods and ZIP codes. Each ZIP item in the list is interactive: clicking it updates the zip URL parameter and triggers a coverage check via the controller. The list also highlights the city/section that contains the ZIP specified in the current URL.

Shows a concise summary of the general local move coverage radius using the local_move_radius_miles value from getServiceAreasList. This provides users with a quick understanding of how far local moves are generally supported without listing any static distances.

layout.grid_system: Implicit 12-column responsive grid with a centered max-width content container (likely around 1140–1200px) and large outer gutters.
layout.layout_pattern: The page follows a linear, top-to-bottom flow with a full-width hero band at the top and stacked sections beneath. A dark, desaturated photographic hero background sits behind a centered, floating nav (from the broader site), with centrally aligned 'Service Areas' hero copy. Directly below the hero, a white background section introduces the ZIP coverage checker: a left-aligned section heading with a large, centered orange card containing the ZIP coverage form. The coverage result panel appears immediately below the form, followed by a compact strip of action links. Further down, a dedicated service-areas section uses a white or very light neutral background, with the service-area list on the left and a local-radius summary card on the right within the same centered content container.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: The page follows a linear, top-to-bottom flow with a full-width hero band at the top and stacked sections beneath. A dark, desaturated photographic hero background sits behind a centered, floating nav (from the broader site), with centrally aligned 'Service Areas' hero copy. Directly below the hero, a white background section introduces the ZIP coverage checker: a left-aligned section heading with a large, centered orange card containing the ZIP coverage form. The coverage result panel appears immediately below the form, followed by a compact strip of action links. Further down, a dedicated service-areas section uses a white or very light neutral background, with the service-area list on the left and a local-radius summary card on the right within the same centered content container.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: The hero occupies roughly the top 45–55% of the viewport height, dominated by the background image and hero text. The ZIP coverage section below uses generous vertical spacing: ~64–96px from the hero to the 'Check coverage' heading, ~24–32px from heading to the orange card, and comfortable 24–32px paddings inside the card. The form card spans about 70–80% of the max-width content container (which itself is roughly 1140–1200px wide and centered), leaving wide lateral gutters. The coverage result panel and action links are compact but separated by 16–32px vertical gaps. The service-areas section below gets another 64–96px top margin and uses an 8/12 to 4/12 width split for list vs. summary, with consistent outer margins and ample vertical rhythm between list groups.
layout_strategies.content_density: Content remains spacious and low-to-medium density. Key interactive elements (ZIP form, action links) are given room to breathe with generous padding and whitespace around them. Text blocks are short and scannable, with clear hierarchy. The service-area list is denser than the hero but still grouped with logical spacing (16–24px between rows/groups) to avoid visual clutter. Cards use internal padding in the 16–24px vertical and 24–32px horizontal range, matching the design DNA.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: The eye starts at the top-left logo in the floating nav, sweeps right across navigation items to a bright CTA, then drops down into the hero's central heading describing 'Service Areas'. From there, the eye is guided downward to the left-aligned ZIP coverage section heading and into the large, high-contrast orange form card centered beneath it. After submitting, attention moves naturally to the coverage result panel directly below, then to the row of action links (quote, booking, or viewing all areas). As the user scrolls further, the eye moves into the service-area list on the left while occasionally referencing the local-radius summary card on the right, creating a left-to-right reading pattern within that section.

layout.overall_description: The Service Areas page opens with a full-bleed, darkened background photo that fills roughly the upper half of the viewport. Floating over the top of this is a white, rounded navigation bar centered horizontally, leaving wide margins to the left and right and casting a soft shadow onto the hero image. In the middle of the hero, the 'Service Areas' heading and a short supporting line are center-aligned, set in bright brand colors against the dark backdrop for strong contrast. As you scroll past this hero band, the background shifts to a clean white. Near the left edge of the main content column, a clear heading introduces the coverage checker, while below it a wide, pill-like orange card is centered in the page, spanning about three-quarters of the content width with generous rounded corners and padding. Inside, the ZIP coverage form is prominent and easy to access. Immediately beneath this card, a result panel appears as a slightly elevated white or very light card aligned to the same center, followed by a centered row of action links that bridge users into quotes or bookings. Further down, a new section introduces the full list of service areas: on larger screens, the page splits into a two-column layout where the left two-thirds are occupied by a structured list of cities and neighborhoods, while the right third hosts a compact, elevated local-radius summary card. The whole layout feels airy and organized, alternating between bold color blocks and white space, while keeping all content aligned within a consistent, centered grid.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The page-init-controller is a non-visual, structural component that conceptually sits at the very top of the page hierarchy, above the hero section. It does not render any visible UI or occupy space in the layout; instead, it initializes data and state for the Service Areas page. From a spatial perspective, it wraps the entire viewport and all subsequent sections (hero, ZIP coverage area, service-area list section), but remains invisible and non-interactive, having no effect on spacing, alignment, or stacking of visible elements.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Non-rendering controller with no visual footprint; it does not participate in the layout flow or affect spacing.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The zip-coverage-form is the primary interactive element of the first content section beneath the hero. It is housed inside a large, horizontally centered orange card that visually echoes the rounded cards from the mockup. This card sits on a white background band. A section heading such as 'Check coverage by ZIP' is placed in the main content column above it, aligned to the left, with about 24–32px of vertical space between the heading and the top of the card. The card itself spans roughly 70–80% of the max-width container (leaving equal left and right margins) and has large corner radii, giving it a friendly, approachable feel. Internally, the form field(s) and submit button are laid out horizontally at desktop widths: the ZIP input occupies about two-thirds of the card's inner width, while the submit button sits to its right occupying the remaining one-third, with 16–24px padding around them. On smaller screens, these elements stack vertically but remain centered. Vertical padding inside the card is generous (24–32px top and bottom), making the form feel substantial but uncluttered. The bottom edge of this orange card aligns horizontally with where the coverage-result-panel will appear, establishing a clear visual connection between the form and its results.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Standard block-level component centered within the main content container; it expands vertically to fit its fields and adjusts from horizontal to stacked layout responsively.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The coverage-result-panel is positioned directly below the zip-coverage-form, within the same white background section and aligned to the same centerline. When visible, it appears as a slightly narrower or equal-width card that mirrors the form card's width (about 70–80% of the content container) and is vertically separated from the form by a modest 16–24px gap. To emphasize hierarchy, this card uses a white or very pale neutral background with a subtle shadow and rounded corners, creating a layered effect beneath the bold orange form card. The panel's text (e.g., 'Yes, you’re covered!' or 'Outside our area') is left-aligned within the card but overall the card itself remains centered in the layout. Internal padding matches the design system (16–24px vertically, 24–32px horizontally), allowing for short explanatory copy and maybe a small icon without feeling crowded. The panel extends only as tall as the content requires, maintaining the low-to-medium content density, and sits immediately above the area-action-links, forming a clear vertical progression from input to feedback to next actions.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Appears and collapses in place under the form; standard block-level behavior. When hidden, the surrounding components (like area-action-links) shift upward in the vertical flow so no empty space remains.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The area-action-links component sits in the same section as the zip-coverage-form and coverage-result-panel, immediately below the result panel or, if no result is shown yet, directly beneath the form card. It is centered horizontally within the content container and typically spans about 50–60% of the page width, ensuring generous white space on either side. Vertically, it is separated from the card above by about 24–32px and from the next section below by 48–64px, reinforcing its role as a bridge between checking coverage and taking further steps. The actions themselves (e.g., 'Request a Quote', 'Book a Move', 'View All Service Areas') are arranged in a single horizontal row on larger screens, using pill-shaped buttons with consistent spacing (16–24px between buttons). On smaller screens, the links stack vertically while remaining center-aligned. The component visually reads as a compact toolbar of CTAs nested within the white background, maintaining the clean, airy aesthetic.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Standard block-level component that remains centered and responsive; flex layout with horizontal alignment on desktop and vertical stacking on smaller viewports.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The service-area-list anchors the main body of the second content section, which appears after a clear break from the ZIP checker section (64–96px vertical spacing). This section uses either a white or very light neutral background and a centered content container. A heading such as 'All Service Areas' is placed at the top of the section, aligned with the left edge of the container. Below this heading, the layout splits into a two-column grid on larger screens: the service-area-list occupies the left 8 of 12 columns (about two-thirds of the container width), while the local-radius-summary sits to the right in the remaining 4 columns. The list is left-aligned and starts near the top of this left column with a small 16–24px gap below the heading. Items might be grouped by region or city, with 8–16px between individual rows and 24–32px between groups, maintaining readability without overwhelming the user. The list scrolls vertically within the page flow; as it grows taller than the viewport, the page scroll bar handles navigation. The right gutter between the list and the summary card is around 32px to maintain clear separation while keeping them visually connected.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Standard block-level component occupying the left portion of a responsive two-column layout; on narrower screens it stacks above the local-radius-summary, occupying 100% of the container width.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The local-radius-summary is positioned within the same service-areas section as the service-area-list, aligned to the right side of the main content container. On larger screens, it occupies the rightmost 4 of 12 columns (about one-third of the container width) and is vertically aligned near the top of the list column, sitting roughly level with the first visible items in the service-area-list. It appears as a compact, elevated card with a white background, rounded corners, and a subtle drop shadow, echoing the card style used for the coverage-result-panel. Inside, succinct copy and possibly a small diagram or icon communicate the typical service radius (e.g., 'We operate within 30 miles of downtown'). The card’s width matches its column; inner padding is 16–24px vertically and 24–32px horizontally. There is a clear 32px gutter between this card and the service-area-list. On smaller screens, this component drops below the list, still centered, spanning the full width of the container with consistent margins on either side. Its visual weight is less than the hero or ZIP card but still noticeable due to elevation and color contrast.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: On larger screens, it may use a sticky behavior within its section—remaining visible as the user scrolls through a taller service-area-list—anchored with a top offset that respects the section heading. On smaller screens, it behaves as a normal block element stacked below the list.

name: FAQ
title: FAQ
description: Page that loads and organizes frequently asked questions about moving services, pricing, promotions, and insurance, allowing users to quickly find answers or navigate to contact support if needed.

core_features: Load FAQ sections and questions from getFaqContent and render them grouped by topic.
Support URL-based deep linking using the `section` parameter to open a specific FAQ section on initial load and when the URL changes.
Support URL-based deep linking using the `q` parameter to scroll to and highlight a specific FAQ question on initial load and when the URL changes.
Provide an in-page keyword filter that narrows visible FAQ questions client-side based on user input, using the data loaded from getFaqContent.
Allow users to expand and collapse FAQ sections and individual questions (accordion behavior) without reloading data.
Allow users to copy or open a direct link to a specific FAQ question by generating a URL with appropriate `section` and `q` parameters derived from the question’s content.
Provide a clear call-to-action link that navigates to contact.html with `topic=other` when the user still needs help after reading the FAQs.
user_workflows:
Browse FAQs by topic:
1. User navigates to faq.html (without specific URL parameters) from the footer or a 'View full FAQ' link.
2. The page calls getFaqContent and receives an array of sections with questions.
3. All sections are listed; either the first section or none is expanded by default (implementation choice), but no section is forced by URL.
4. User clicks a section header to expand it and see its questions.
5. User clicks a question to expand its answer.
6. User reads answers and can move between sections without additional network requests.

Open FAQ with a specific section from other pages:
1. User clicks a link on index.html, booking.html, promotions.html, or insurance.html that points to faq.html with a `section` parameter.
2. On load, the FAQ page parses the `section` parameter from the URL.
3. The page calls getFaqContent.
4. After data is loaded, the page searches the returned sections for one whose `id` matches the `section` parameter.
5. If found, that section is set as expanded and scrolled into view.
6. If not found, the page falls back to a default state (e.g., no specific section forced) while still showing all sections for browsing.

Open FAQ with a specific question from the home page:
1. User clicks a link on index.html that points to faq.html with `section` and/or `q` parameters.
2. On load, the FAQ page parses `section` and `q` from the URL.
3. The page calls getFaqContent.
4. After data is loaded, the page:
   - Uses `section` (if present) to expand the matching section.
   - Uses `q` to locate a target question by comparing `q` to each question’s generated anchor (for example, a slug derived from the question text).
5. If a match is found, the page automatically expands that question, scrolls it into view, and applies a temporary visual highlight state.
6. If no matching question is found, the page still opens the requested section (if valid) and leaves other sections accessible.

Find answers using keyword search within the FAQ:
1. User navigates to faq.html and the page loads FAQ content via getFaqContent.
2. User types a keyword or phrase into the FAQ search/filter input.
3. On each change, the page filters the in-memory list of questions and identifies those whose question text and/or answer text contain the entered keywords.
4. The UI updates to show only sections that contain at least one matching question, and within those sections only the matching questions are visible (or are emphasized).
5. As the user clears or changes the search query, the list of sections and questions updates in real time.
6. The search is local; no additional API calls are made.

Contact support when the answer is not found:
1. After browsing sections and/or using the search, the user reaches the bottom of the FAQ page.
2. The user clicks the "Still have questions? Contact us" call-to-action.
3. The page navigates to contact.html with the query parameter `topic=other`.
4. The contact page uses this topic parameter to preselect or prefill the relevant topic field (handled by the contact page).
interactions: On page load, trigger a call to getFaqContent. While the request is pending, show a loading state. If it fails, show a retry option without revealing any static FAQ content.
Clicking a section header toggles the expanded/collapsed state of that section. Expanding a section may collapse others depending on the chosen accordion behavior, but no network call is repeated.
Clicking a question row toggles the visibility of its answer text. When opening a question, the component ensures its parent section is expanded.
Typing into the search/filter input updates the internal `searchQuery` state and triggers client-side filtering of the loaded FAQ questions, without calling getFaqContent again.
Clearing the search input resets the filter state and restores the default view of all sections and questions.

Clicking a "link to this question" control (e.g., an icon or text in each question row) generates a URL to faq.html with `section` set to the containing section’s `id` and `q` set to the question’s generated anchor. The URL is either copied to clipboard, shown to the user, or applied to the browser’s address bar using the History API, without reloading the page.

When the browser’s address bar URL changes (for example, user navigates back/forward), the page re-parses `section` and `q` parameters and updates the expanded section and highlighted question accordingly, using the data already loaded from getFaqContent if available.
Clicking the "Still have questions? Contact us" link triggers navigation to contact.html with `topic=other` as a query parameter.

If the user arrives with only a `q` parameter and no `section`, the page attempts to locate the question by anchor globally across all sections after loading the FAQs, expands the relevant section, and scrolls to that question.
page_functionality.state_logic: The FAQ page state is driven by the result of getFaqContent and the URL query parameters `section` and `q`. On initialization: (1) The page parses `section` and `q` from the current URL. (2) It calls getFaqContent and stores the returned sections and questions in memory. (3) After data arrives, an initialization routine runs: if `section` is present, the page looks for a section with a matching `id` and marks it as `expandedSectionId`; otherwise, `expandedSectionId` is null or a default section. (4) For each question, the page calculates a question anchor (e.g., by transforming the question text into a URL-safe slug) and uses this to compare with the `q` parameter. If `q` matches a question anchor, the page sets `focusedQuestionAnchor` to that anchor, marks that question as expanded, ensures its parent section is expanded, and scrolls it into view with a temporary `highlighted = true` state. If no match is found, `focusedQuestionAnchor` remains null. The page also maintains `searchQuery`, representing the current filter text; when `searchQuery` is non-empty, the visible sections and questions are derived as a filtered subset of the loaded content, while the underlying data remains unchanged. The URL is updated when the user explicitly interacts with deep-link controls (e.g., 'link to this question') by setting or updating `section` and `q` parameters using the History API, without triggering a full reload. However, if the user arrives or navigates via a URL change (including back/forward navigation) that modifies these parameters, the same initialization logic is re-applied against the already-loaded FAQ data (or, if necessary, after re-calling getFaqContent if the page was fully reloaded). No state is stored in localStorage; all state is kept in memory and/or expressed via URL parameters.

components.functionality: Responsible for calling getFaqContent on page initialization, handling loading and error states, and exposing the loaded FAQ sections and questions to child components.
event_handlers: onMount: Call getFaqContent and set local state { sections, isLoading, error }.
onRetryClick: Re-call getFaqContent if the previous attempt failed.
onLoadSuccess: Pass loaded sections to FAQ display components and trigger initial URL-parameter-based selection (section/q).
onLoadError: Update error state so an error message and retry control can be shown.

components.functionality: Manages synchronization between URL parameters (`section`, `q`) and the internal FAQ state (expanded section, focused question). Parses parameters on load and when the URL changes, and provides helper methods to generate deep links.
event_handlers: onInitAfterDataLoad: Read `section` and `q` from URL, derive `expandedSectionId` and `focusedQuestionAnchor`, and notify the FAQ list component.
onUrlChange: Re-parse `section` and `q` when the browser history changes (back/forward) and adjust expanded section and focused question accordingly.
onSetSection: Update `section` parameter in the URL when a user explicitly navigates to a section, using History API without page reload.
onSetQuestionAnchor: Update both `section` and `q` parameters to point to a specific question, using History API without page reload.

components.functionality: Provides a text input to filter FAQ questions by keywords. Produces a `searchQuery` value that is used to compute a filtered view of sections and questions on the client side.
event_handlers: onChange: Update `searchQuery` as the user types.
onClear: Reset `searchQuery` to an empty value and restore the unfiltered FAQ view.
onSubmit: Prevent full page form submission and rely on real-time filtering instead.

components.functionality: Displays all FAQ sections returned by getFaqContent as an accordion. Handles expanding and collapsing sections, and delegates question-specific behavior to child components. Respects `expandedSectionId` and `searchQuery` to determine which sections and questions are visible.
event_handlers: onSectionHeaderClick: Toggle the expansion state of the clicked section, and notify faq-url-state-manager so it can optionally update the `section` URL parameter.
onQuestionToggleRequest: Ensure the parent section of a question is expanded before toggling the question’s answer visibility.
onReceiveFocusedQuestionAnchor: When notified of a `focusedQuestionAnchor`, expand the relevant section, expand the question, and request scrolling into view.

components.functionality: Represents a single question-and-answer pair within a section. Controls expansion of the answer, highlighting when it matches the `q` URL parameter, and generation of a deep link URL to this specific question.
event_handlers: onHeaderClick: Toggle internal `isExpanded` state for the question, revealing or hiding the answer.

onDeepLinkClick: Generate a URL to faq.html with `section` equal to the parent section’s `id` and `q` equal to this question’s generated anchor; call faq-url-state-manager.onSetQuestionAnchor to update the URL without reloading.
onAnchorMatch: When the question’s anchor matches `focusedQuestionAnchor`, automatically expand the question, highlight it, and scroll it into view.

components.functionality: Optional compact navigation (such as a list or menu) of FAQ sections that lets users jump directly to a section. It uses section IDs from getFaqContent and keeps its active state in sync with `expandedSectionId`.
event_handlers: onNavItemClick: Set the clicked section as the active section, request that faq-section-list expand it, and call faq-url-state-manager.onSetSection to update the `section` parameter in the URL.
onExpandedSectionChange: When `expandedSectionId` changes (for example, via accordion interaction), update the active item in this navigation list.

components.functionality: Provides a call-to-action control that sends users to the contact page when they do not find the answer they need in the FAQs.
event_handlers: onClick: Navigate to contact.html with the query parameter `topic=other` (i.e., contact.html?topic=other).

layout.grid_system: Implicit 12-column responsive grid with a centered max-width content container (likely around 1140–1200px) and large outer gutters.
layout.layout_pattern: Use a top-to-bottom linear flow that mirrors the mockup: global floating nav at the top, followed by a full-width hero with a darkened photographic background and centered hero copy specific to FAQs. Below the hero, transition into a white-background content section. Inside this section, place the FAQ search/filter at the top center, then organize the FAQ tools into a two-column layout on desktop (left: section navigation, right: accordion list of questions) while keeping everything centered within a max-width container. Conclude with a prominent, centered contact CTA anchored at the bottom of the FAQ content.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: Use a top-to-bottom linear flow that mirrors the mockup: global floating nav at the top, followed by a full-width hero with a darkened photographic background and centered hero copy specific to FAQs. Below the hero, transition into a white-background content section. Inside this section, place the FAQ search/filter at the top center, then organize the FAQ tools into a two-column layout on desktop (left: section navigation, right: accordion list of questions) while keeping everything centered within a max-width container. Conclude with a prominent, centered contact CTA anchored at the bottom of the FAQ content.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: Reserve the upper 45–55% of the first viewport for the hero area, dominated by the background image and centered FAQ heading/subheading. Beneath that, the FAQ body occupies the remaining vertical space as a stacked section with generous top and bottom padding (64–96px). Within the FAQ content container (max-width ~1140–1200px), allocate roughly 25–30% width to the section navigation and 70–75% to the accordion list on desktop. On tablet and mobile, stack these vertically at full width. Maintain wide lateral gutters so all content sits comfortably in the central column.
layout_strategies.content_density: Maintain low-to-medium content density consistent with the mockup: large tap targets, 16–24px internal padding for cards and interactive elements, 24–32px spacing between functional blocks (search, navigation, accordion list, CTA), and 48–64px breathing room around major section headings. FAQ items themselves are concise and broken into expandable cards, preventing visual clutter despite many questions.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: Guide the eye from the site logo and header CTA in the floating nav, down to the central hero heading like 'Moving FAQ' in a strong brand color. From there, lead users to the search/filter field at the top of the white FAQ section, then into the section navigation (for choosing a topic) and across to the accordion list (reading answers). Finally, draw attention to the bottom contact CTA with a high-contrast orange button set in ample whitespace, inviting users to reach out if their question isn’t answered.

layout.overall_description: The FAQ page opens with the familiar floating, rounded navigation bar centered near the top of the viewport, sitting above a darkened full-width hero photo of a moving-related scene. The hero fills about half the initial viewport height, with a bold orange 'FAQs' or 'Moving Questions, Answered' headline and a short white subheading centered both horizontally and vertically in the hero area. As you scroll past the hero, the background shifts to a clean white band that houses all FAQ content within a centered max-width container. At the top of this white section, a left-aligned section title like 'Frequently Asked Questions' anchors the content, while immediately beneath it a wide, pill-like search bar is centered and invites users to filter questions. Below the search, the content splits into two columns on desktop: a vertical list of FAQ sections on the left in a slim, white card column, and a broader column of stacked accordion cards on the right containing the actual questions and answers. The left navigation remains visible as you scroll through the questions, while the right column extends downward with expandable cards that echo the rounded, softly elevated style from the hero’s floating nav. After the last FAQ item, the layout recenters into a single column with generous vertical spacing, culminating in a bright orange, rounded button-style link that invites users to contact the moving company for more help, set against plenty of white space to emphasize it as the final call to action.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: This non-visible data-loader component manifests only when the FAQ content is being fetched or refreshed. When active, it overlays the entire viewport with a semi-transparent dark scrim that subtly blurs or mutes the underlying hero and FAQ content, matching the darkened photographic aesthetic of the hero. At the center of the screen, it displays a small, white, rounded rectangle card (approximately 280–320px wide) with 24px padding all around, a simple spinner or progress indicator, and a short label like 'Loading FAQs…' in a neutral text color. The card is horizontally and vertically centered in the viewport and uses a soft shadow to subtly float above the scrim, visually echoing the floating nav bar’s elevation. The overlay covers the nav, hero, and FAQ content so the user clearly understands the page is in a loading state.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Fixed full-screen overlay that sits above all other content when active (z-index high), centered in the viewport and not affected by scrolling.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: This state-controller component operates entirely behind the scenes and does not render any visible UI. Functionally, it connects the URL (query parameters or hash) to the currently selected FAQ section and open question, ensuring that when a user clicks a section in the left navigation or expands a question, the URL reflects that state and can be shared or reloaded. Visually, its influence is seen in which section appears highlighted in the section navigation and which accordion items are expanded, but it occupies no spatial footprint itself.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Non-visual, no spatial behavior; it simply orchestrates state for the other components without affecting layout.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The FAQ section filter appears at the top of the white FAQ content band, just below the hero area and section heading. It is centered within the main content container and spans roughly 60–70% of the container width, with a max-width around 640px so it feels substantial but not overwhelming. There is generous vertical space above and below it: approximately 32–40px margin below the section heading and 24–32px margin before the section navigation and FAQ list begin. The filter itself is a horizontally oriented search field with a large pill or rounded rectangle shape (border-radius around 24–32px), a subtle light border, and a clear left-aligned search icon inside. It is about 48–56px tall, with 16px vertical and 24px horizontal internal padding. On larger screens, it sits perfectly centered, with wide white gutters on both sides, reinforcing the low-density, marketing-style layout. On tablets and mobile, it still sits at the top of the FAQ content stack, spanning almost the full width minus the standard page margins, maintaining a visually prominent position before the navigation and questions.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static element in the normal document flow, shifting from centered narrow width on large screens to nearly full-width on smaller screens while always anchored at the top of the FAQ content section.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The FAQ section list occupies the main right-hand column of the FAQ content area on desktop, holding the expandable accordion items. Starting about 24–32px below the search filter, the content divides into two columns within the centered container: the section navigation on the left and this section list on the right. The section list column spans roughly 70–75% of the container width, aligning its left edge with the centerline of the page and extending toward the right gutter, but still leaving comfortable outer margins. Within this column, FAQ questions appear as a vertical stack of full-width accordion cards, each separated by 16–24px of vertical spacing to maintain a roomy feel. The list can extend far down the page, and as users scroll, the questions move upward while the left navigation remains visible (on supported screens), emphasizing that this right column is where browsing and reading primarily happen. On tablet and mobile, once the layout collapses to a single column, the section list appears directly beneath the section navigation, occupying the full width of the content column with consistent padding from the screen edges, mirroring the desktop’s central alignment.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Standard scrollable column within the page flow. It grows vertically with content and, on desktop, sits to the right of the section navigation; on smaller breakpoints, it shifts below the navigation in a single-column stack.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: Each FAQ question item is a full-width accordion card within the section list column, styled to echo the mockup’s rounded, lightly elevated containers. The card stretches the full width of its column, with 16–20px vertical and 24–32px horizontal internal padding. It has large rounded corners (around 16–20px radius), a clean white background, a subtle soft shadow, and a thin, low-contrast border to distinguish it from the white page background without feeling heavy. The top portion of the card is the question header row, approximately 56–64px tall, with the question text left-aligned in a medium-to-large type size and a caret or plus/minus icon on the far right. The entire header row is clickable and has sufficient vertical padding to make it comfortable for tapping. When the item is expanded, the answer text appears directly beneath the question header within the same card, separated by a thin divider or slight background tint. The answer body has 16–20px padding on all sides and uses a medium line height for easy reading. The active/open state can be indicated by an orange accent—such as a 3–4px orange bar along the left edge or an orange-tinted icon—while inactive cards remain neutral. Vertically, 16–24px gaps between cards maintain the spacious, low-density feeling seen in the mockup.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Accordion behavior within the list: expands and collapses vertically, pushing subsequent items down when open. It remains anchored in place in the column, with no fixed or sticky behavior.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The FAQ section navigation sits directly below the search filter and to the left of the accordion list on desktop, forming the left column of the main FAQ content area. After a small vertical gap (around 24–32px) below the filter, the content splits into two columns: this navigation column on the left occupying roughly 25–30% of the container width, and the section list on the right. The navigation itself is a vertically stacked list of section labels (e.g., 'General', 'Pricing', 'Promotions', 'Insurance'), each presented as a rounded pill or compact card with 12–16px vertical and 20–24px horizontal padding. The column begins near the top of the FAQ content area and extends downward as needed, with 8–16px vertical spacing between each navigation item. The active/selected section uses a strong brand color (orange or green) for its background or left accent bar, while inactive items remain white or light gray with a subtle border, maintaining a clean, corporate look. On desktop and large tablets, the entire navigation column is aligned flush left within the content container, maintaining generous space between it and the section list.
